Invariant: `seqNum` must increment monotonically for a write call, see table above","params":{"caller":"Caller's address","seqNumAct":"Received sequence number","seqNumExp":"Expected sequence number"}}],"UUPSUnauthorizedCallContext()":[{"details":"The call is from an unauthorized context."}],"UUPSUnsupportedProxiableUUID(bytes32)":[{"details":"The storage `slot` is unsupported as a UUID."}]},"events":{"Initialized(uint64)":{"details":"Triggered when the contract has been initialized or reinitialized."},"ReqAck(bytes16,address,uint40,(bytes16,uint16,uint16,uint16,uint40,uint40),bool)":{"details":"A Request Acknowledgement Event occurs at the end of each request (if no revert) and bridges the gaps between data sources, clients, lossy/unreliable protocols/providers/neworks, and the on-chain call outcome - Captures 3 categories of data for off-chain calls:     - Key inputs (indexed params) allow a tx search via either:         - PK1: reqId: Allows a tx search on a single field such as to fill a gap on replay         - PK2: (caller,seqNum): Allows a tx search to audit a sequence for a caller     - Context: `replay` and `callRes.blockNum` provide context on when/where this happened     - Output: `callRes` provides fields (rc, lrc, count) for either a simple result or summary         - Additional results can be found in the tx receipt logs from prior events in the same tx","params":{"callRes":"See `CallRes`; `reqId` and `ReqAck` are separate params for indexes","caller":"Account initiating the tx","replay":"false for an original event, true when `reqId` was already processed","reqId":"From CallRes: Request ID, allows for gap-fill on replay","seqNum":"From CallRes: Caller's sequence number incremented monotonically"}},"Upgraded(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the implementation is upgraded."}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"addInstEarnDate(uint40,bytes16,string,uint256)":{"custom:api":"public","details":"Add an instrument earn date to each map, duplicates ignored","params":{"earnDate":"Instrument earn date","instName":"Instrument name","reqId":"Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers","seqNumEx":"=0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`"}},"constructor":{"custom:api":"private","details":"Ensures the logic contract cannot be hijacked before the `initializer` runs - This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. addInstEarnDate(uint40,bytes16,string,ui.details: Add an instrument earn date to each map, duplicates ignored

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

constructor.details: Ensures the logic contract cannot be hijacked before the `initializer` runs - Sets version to `type(uint64).max` + `emit Initialized(version)` to prevent future initialization - `initialize` is where the business logic is initialized on proxies - For more info see comments in 'Initializable.sol'

getCallResByReqId(bytes16).details: Get a result for a prior write function call invoked from off-chain by this caller - Subject to storage lag when retrieved off-chain shortly after write, prefer the `ReqAck` event in that case

params.reqId: Request ID of a prior call

getCallResBySeqNum(uint40).details: Get a result for a prior write function call invoked from off-chain by this caller - Subject to storage lag when retrieved off-chain shortly after write, prefer the `ReqAck` event in that case

params.seqNum: Sequence number of a prior call by this caller

params.count: Items to get, 0 = [iBegin:] (may exceed gas), See `getEarnDatesLen()` and PAGE_REQUESTS.
params.iBegin: Index in the array to begin processing

params.count: Items to get, 0 = [iBegin:] (may exceed gas), See `getEarnDatesForInstLen(instName)` and PAGE_REQUESTS.
params.iBegin: Index in the array to begin processing

getEarnDatesForInstLen(string).details: Get count of instrument's earn dates

getEarnDatesLen().details: Get count of all earn dates

getInstNames(uint256,uint256).details: Get all instrument names - GET_INST_NAME_GAS: Storage is optimized at the expense of retrieval conversion but seems sufficient at   ~6.27K with count=200 using a solidity string conversion (vs assembly).

params.count: Items to get, 0 = [iBegin:] (may exceed gas), See `getInstNamesLen()` and PAGE_REQUESTS.
params.iBegin: Index in the array to begin processing

getInstNamesForDate(uint256,uint256,uint.details: Get instrument names for an earn date, see GET_INST_NAME_GAS

params.count: Items to get, 0 = [iBegin:] (may exceed gas), See `getInstNamesForDateLen(earnDate)` and PAGE_REQUESTS.
params.iBegin: Index in the array to begin processing

getInstNamesForDateLen(uint256).details: Get count of instrument names for an earn date

getInstNamesLen().details: Get count of all instrument names

getSeqNum(address).details: Get next expected seq num for the given account address

returns.seqNumEx: Next expected seq num to be passed to a write function call from `account`

initialize(address,bytes16).details: Basically replaces the constructor in a proxy oriented contract - `initializer` modifier ensures this function can only be called once during deploy - See UUPS_UPGRADE_SEQ for details on how to upgrade this contract

params.creator: Creator's address for access control during setup
params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers

preUpgrade(uint40,bytes16,bytes16).details: Stage values to use in the next call to `__authorizeUpgrade`

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC-1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

removeInstEarnDate(uint40,bytes16,string.details: Remove an instrument earn date from relevant maps, ignores unknown

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

documentation.text: @notice Access control; allow RevMgr or Creator

@dev Upgrade hook to enforce permissions
 - Ensure `preUpgrade` is called before this function to stage required params
